attributes.description: AWS EC2 dashboard for CloudWatch metrics collected by the OpenTelemetry Collector.
attributes.title: [AWS OTel] EC2

embeddableConfig.description:
Monitor AWS EC2 instances across your infrastructure in real time.
Use the filters at the top to narrow by instance, region, or account. Spot outliers instantly in the summary table — click any instance to filter the dashboard.
To get started, instrument your EC2 instances with the Elastic agent.
Total number of EC2 instances — distinct instances reporting metrics within a 5-minute freshness window.

attributes.description: Total number of EC2 instances — distinct instances reporting metrics within a 5-minute freshness window.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

embeddableConfig.title: Instances by status
embeddableConfig.description: Share of EC2 instances by latest status-check result — impaired (StatusCheckFailed > 0) vs healthy (StatusCheckFailed = 0 or absent). Uses the most recent value per instance within a 5-minute freshness window. A growing impaired slice indicates spreading status-check failures.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY InstanceId, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| EVAL ec2_state = CASE(
    failed_recent > 0, "impaired",
    failed_recent == 0, "healthy",
    "Unknown")
| STATS instances = COUNT(*) BY ec2_state

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY InstanceId, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| EVAL ec2_state = CASE(
    failed_recent > 0, "impaired",
    failed_recent == 0, "healthy",
    "Unknown")
| STATS instances = COUNT(*) BY ec2_state

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
| STATS failed_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)))
  BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), InstanceId, cloud.region
| EVAL state = CASE(
    failed_max > 0, "impaired",
    failed_max == 0, "healthy",
    "Unknown")
| STATS total = COUNT(*),
    impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "impaired",
    healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy",
    unknown = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "Unknown"
  BY timestamp
| SORT timestamp ASC

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
| STATS failed_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)))
  BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), InstanceId, cloud.region
| EVAL state = CASE(
    failed_max > 0, "impaired",
    failed_max == 0, "healthy",
    "Unknown")
| STATS total = COUNT(*),
    impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "impaired",
    healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy",
    unknown = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "Unknown"
  BY timestamp
| SORT timestamp ASC

embeddableConfig.title: EC2 instance count over time
embeddableConfig.description: Count of EC2 instances over time, classified per time bucket as impaired (StatusCheckFailed > 0), healthy (= 0), or no data (metric missing). Distinct instances are counted per bucket, so totals track how many instances reported in each interval. A rising impaired series indicates spreading failures.

Number of impaired EC2 instances — distinct instances whose latest StatusCheckFailed is greater than 0, within a 5-minute freshness window. A non-zero count indicates instances failing EC2 status checks and likely unreachable.

attributes.description: Number of impaired EC2 instances — distinct instances whose latest StatusCheckFailed is greater than 0, within a 5-minute freshness window. A non-zero count indicates instances failing EC2 status checks and likely unreachable.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" AND stat == "Maximum" | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)), last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent > 0 | KEEP impaired

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" AND stat == "Maximum" | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)), last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent > 0 | KEEP impaired

embeddableConfig.description: Number of healthy EC2 instances — distinct instances whose latest StatusCheckFailed is 0 or absent, within a 5-minute freshness window. Absence of a StatusCheckFailed metric is treated as passing, matching the CloudWatch console.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY InstanceId, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| STATS healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent == 0
| KEEP healthy

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY InstanceId, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| STATS healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent == 0
| KEEP healthy

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat IN ("Average", "Maximum")
| STATS c_failed_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL))),
  c_cpu = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
  c_ebs_write_ops = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSWriteOps`, NULL))),
  c_dr = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSReadBytes`, NULL))),
  c_dw = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSWriteBytes`, NULL))),
  c_ni = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkIn`, NULL))),
  c_no = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut`, NULL))),
  last_ms = MAX(TO_LONG(@timestamp))
  BY InstanceId, c_account = cloud.account.id, c_region = cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_ms = MAX(last_ms)
| WHERE (latest_ms - last_ms) <= 300000
| EVAL c_cpu = COALESCE(c_cpu, 0.0),
  c_ebs_write_ops = COALESCE(c_ebs_write_ops, 0.0),
  c_dr = COALESCE(c_dr, 0.0),
  c_dw = COALESCE(c_dw, 0.0),
  c_ni = COALESCE(c_ni, 0.0),
  c_no = COALESCE(c_no, 0.0)
| EVAL c_state_sort = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, 1, c_failed_max == 0, 2, 3),
  c_state = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, "impaired", c_failed_max == 0, "healthy", "Unknown")
| SORT c_state_sort ASC

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat IN ("Average", "Maximum")
| STATS c_failed_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL))),
  c_cpu = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
  c_ebs_write_ops = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSWriteOps`, NULL))),
  c_dr = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSReadBytes`, NULL))),
  c_dw = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSWriteBytes`, NULL))),
  c_ni = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkIn`, NULL))),
  c_no = AVG(LAST_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut`, NULL))),
  last_ms = MAX(TO_LONG(@timestamp))
  BY InstanceId, c_account = cloud.account.id, c_region = cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_ms = MAX(last_ms)
| WHERE (latest_ms - last_ms) <= 300000
| EVAL c_cpu = COALESCE(c_cpu, 0.0),
  c_ebs_write_ops = COALESCE(c_ebs_write_ops, 0.0),
  c_dr = COALESCE(c_dr, 0.0),
  c_dw = COALESCE(c_dw, 0.0),
  c_ni = COALESCE(c_ni, 0.0),
  c_no = COALESCE(c_no, 0.0)
| EVAL c_state_sort = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, 1, c_failed_max == 0, 2, 3),
  c_state = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, "impaired", c_failed_max == 0, "healthy", "Unknown")
| SORT c_state_sort ASC

embeddableConfig.title: EC2 instances
embeddableConfig.description: All EC2 instances, ranked impaired first (worst first). Click an instance to drill into its metrics. State reflects the latest StatusCheckFailed within a 5-minute freshness window (impaired > 0, healthy = 0, otherwise no data). Max CPU is the peak utilization (%) over the selected time range; EBS-write-ops and network/EBS byte columns are averaged per CloudWatch period over the range.

drilldowns.label: Open this instance in the AWS console

embeddableConfig.title: EC2 instances — hexagon map (click an hex for actions)
embeddableConfig.description: One hexagon per EC2 instance, ordered impaired then healthy then no-data (worst first). Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or EBS writes (MB per CloudWatch period); metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that instance.

savedVis.title: EC2 instances — hexagon map
savedVis.description: One hexagon per EC2 instance, ordered impaired then healthy then no-data (worst first). Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or EBS writes (MB per CloudWatch period); metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that instance.

embeddableConfig.description: Total inbound network traffic (bytes) across all EC2 instances, summed over the selected time range. Grows as the range widens.

embeddableConfig.title: Top 10 EC2 instances by CPU utilization
embeddableConfig.description: Top 10 EC2 instances by peak CPU utilization (%) over the selected time range (maximum per instance). Reference line at 80% marks the high-CPU threshold; bars crossing it indicate sustained CPU pressure that can throttle workloads.

attributes.description: Top 10 EC2 instances by peak CPU utilization (%) over the selected time range (maximum per instance). Reference line at 80% marks the high-CPU threshold; bars crossing it indicate sustained CPU pressure that can throttle workloads.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | WHERE stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_cpu = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY InstanceId, cloud.region | SORT peak_cpu DESC | LIMIT 10

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | WHERE stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_cpu = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY InstanceId, cloud.region | SORT peak_cpu DESC | LIMIT 10

embeddableConfig.title: Top 10 EC2 instances by EBS write operations
embeddableConfig.description: EBS write operations per instance over time — average EBSWriteOps per CloudWatch period (averaged per time bucket). Top 10 instances by write ops per bucket. Rising values indicate increasing write activity to attached storage.

attributes.description: EBS write operations per instance over time — average EBSWriteOps per CloudWatch period (averaged per time bucket). Top 10 instances by write ops per bucket. Rising values indicate increasing write activity to attached storage.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSWriteOps` IS NOT NULL | WHERE stat == "Sum" | STATS metric = AVG(avg_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/EBSWriteOps`)) BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), InstanceId, cloud.region | SORT timestamp ASC, metric DESC, InstanceId ASC | LIMIT 10 BY timestamp

embeddableConfig.description: Number of EC2 instances reporting metrics but with no StatusCheckFailed signal in the latest 5-minute window (shown as “no data”). A high count can indicate instances not emitting status-check metrics rather than truly healthy ones.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY InstanceId, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| STATS no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent IS NULL
| KEEP no_data

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != ""
  AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY InstanceId, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| STATS no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent IS NULL
| KEEP no_data

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | WHERE stat == "Sum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut` IS NOT NULL | STATS col_no = AVG(avg_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut`)) BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), InstanceId, cloud.region | SORT timestamp ASC, col_no DESC, InstanceId ASC | LIMIT 10 BY timestamp

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | WHERE stat == "Sum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut` IS NOT NULL | STATS col_no = AVG(avg_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut`)) BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), InstanceId, cloud.region | SORT timestamp ASC, col_no DESC, InstanceId ASC | LIMIT 10 BY timestamp

attributes.title: Top 10 EC2 instances by network out
